attacking_style: Opportunistic rather than aggressive; targets bonus seconds and positioning moves (23km out at BinckBank) but rarely initiates decisive attacks in Monument finales even when well-positioned.
team_tactics: Transitioned from protected leader (BMC 2018) to domestique role (EF 2019), suggesting teams recognized declining finishing capacity. Limited evidence of dedicated team setup in recent years.
terrain_preference: Classic Flemish parcours (Roubaix sectors, Flanders bergs) where positioning matters, but lacks explosive power to capitalize on selective climbs despite being well-placed through them.
vulnerability: Finale execution and tactical passivity; repeatedly reaches final groups but fails to animate or challenge for podium. Early crashes can completely eliminate race prospects (2018 Flanders).
key_patterns: Tactically passive in finales despite strong positioning; multiple observations of presence without podium challenge
Form decline trajectory: protected leader 2017-2018 to domestique role by 2019 Flanders
Reaches decisive selections but fades in final sectors/kilometers across multiple Monuments
Bonus hunting and positioning moves early, but rarely follows through with decisive attacks
form_trajectory: negative: Clear regression from pre-race favorite (2018) to domestique duties (2019) with consistent fade pattern in Monument finales. No podium threats in most recent observations.
